---
title: "Sounds, Phonemes, Words"
---

| Date | Topic | Reading | Resources |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | 
| Tues 9/22  | Sounds | SGN Ch. 3, *Sounds in the Brain* (p. 44 - 51) | |
| Thurs 9/24 | Lateralization of speech processing | Zatorre et al. 1992 |  |
| Tues 9/29 | Categorical perception | SGN Ch. 4, *A neural code for speech* (p. 64   - 71) |
| Thurs 10/1 | Phonemes in the brain | Phillips et al. 2000 | |
| Tues 10/6 | Words | SGN Ch. 5: *Activating Words* | |
| Thurs 10/8 | Time course of word processing |  Marinkovic et al. (2003) | |
| Tues 10/13 | <span class="exam-badge">Exam #2</span> | |

::: {.callout-tip}
# Learning Goals: Unit 2

**Sounds & Phonemes**

1. Define the following terms: *phoneme*, *phonetic feature*, *contrastive*, *categorical perception*, *speech envelope*, *entrainment*
1. Identify the major brain regions that are involved in processing language sounds using neuroanatomical and directional terms
1. Point to these regions on a picture of the brain
1. Describe the evidence for the following claim: Speech perception is categorical.
1. Explain what pure word deafness is
1. Describe evidence relevant to the question: Is speech special?

**Words**

1. Define the following terms: *lexical item*, *morpheme*, *supramodal*
1. Identify the stages involved in processing spoken and written words
1. Identify the major brain regions that are involved in processing words using neuroanatomical and directional terms
1. Describe evidence for the following claim: Spoken and written words activate a shared lexical-semantic processing system in the brain
:::
